g.text: GPTQ vs Naive: Weight Compensation Visualized
MSE: 3.6e-5 (64x Improvement) | PPL Δ: +2% | Quality: PRESERVED
# W: Weight matrix, H_inv: Inverse Hessian
# 1. Quantize column j using standard scale
# 2. Compute quantization error for this column
# 3. Use Hessian inverse to update future columns
# This compensates for error_j in the remaining weights
W[:, j+1:] -= error_j.outer(H_inv[j, j+1:]) / H_inv[j, j]

text.tspan: # W: Weight matrix, H_inv: Inverse Hessian
# 1. Quantize column j using standard scale
# 2. Compute quantization error for this column
# 3. Use Hessian inverse to update future columns
# This compensates for error_j in the remaining weights
W[:, j+1:] -= error_j.outer(H_inv[j, j+1:]) / H_inv[j, j]

g.text: Key Principle: Hessian-based compensation redistributes quantization error from column 'j' into the remaining columns 'j+1...N' such that the total output reconstruction error is mathematically minimized.
